paper.title: Identifying Sagittarius Stream Stars By Their APOGEE Chemical Abundance Signatures

extraction.summary: The paper reports one hypervelocity star (HVS) candidate among 68 chemically selected Sgr stream candidates. The candidate's APOGEE ID is listed in Table 1, which is only partially available in the provided source files, preventing extraction of an individual identifier. The extraction is partial until the full table is accessible.

method_chain.summary: APOGEE DR14 spectroscopic data (stellar parameters, chemical abundances, radial velocities for ~176,000 red giants) and Gaia DR2 proper motions.

source_refs.context: Description of APOGEE DR14 data and sample selection criteria.

method_chain.summary: Apply quality and parameter cuts: 3550 < Teff < 4200 K, S/N > 70 per half resolution element, no ASPCAPBAD flag, [Fe/H] > -1.2.

source_refs.context: Text defining the selection criteria for the MW sample.

method_chain.summary: K-means clustering in 6-dimensional chemical space ([(C+N)/Fe], [O/Fe], [Mg/Fe], [Al/Fe], [Mn/Fe], [Ni/Fe]) using k=16 clusters to identify stars with Sgr-like abundances. The cluster containing 90% of the Sgr core sample plus 68 additional stars defines the Sgr stream candidates.

parameters.name: other
parameters.description: Number of clusters chosen based on minimizing split of Sgr core sample.

source_refs.context: Description of k-means clustering and selection of k=16.
K-means clustering method and selection of k clusters.

method_chain.summary: Cross-match the 68 chemical candidates with Gaia DR2 using nearest neighbor within 1 arcsec to obtain proper motions. Of 68, 62 have Gaia matches.

source_refs.context: Text describing Gaia proper motion matching for the 68 chemical candidates.

method_chain.summary: Adopt spectro-photometric distances from the APOGEE DR14 value-added catalog (Hayden+2014; NMSU_dist).

source_refs.context: ... distances are derived by methods described in Hayden+2014 and are part of a value added catalog.

method_chain.summary: Adopt APOGEE DR14 radial velocities for the candidates.

source_refs.context: APOGEE radial velocities supplement with proper motions.

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR2 proper motions for the matched candidates. The proper motion values are adopted directly from the Gaia catalog.

source_refs.context: We supplement the APOGEE ... with proper motions from Gaia DR2.

method_chain.summary: Assume Galactic constants and solar motion: R0 = 8.0 kpc, circular velocity at Sun V_circ = 220 km/s, solar motion from Schoenrich+2010 (U_sun, V_sun, W_sun).

source_refs.context: ... assume a circular velocity of 220 km/s at a Solar radius of 8.0 kpc from the Galactic center, and the Solar motion as measured by Schoenrich+2010.
circular velocity of 220 km/s
Solar motion as measured by Schoenrich+2010.
Solar V velocity from Schoenrich+2010.
Solar W velocity from Schoenrich+2010.
We assume a circular velocity of 220 km/s at a Solar radius of 8.0 kpc from the Galactic center, and the Solar motion as measured by Schoenrich+2010.

method_chain.summary: Transform observed positions, distances, proper motions, and radial velocities to Galactocentric Cartesian coordinates and velocities (UVW) using the assumed solar position and motion.
outputs: Galactocentric positions and velocities for each candidate

source_refs.context: We follow the galpy usage of a left-handed Galactic Cartesian coordinate (velocity) system ...

method_chain.summary: Two gravitational potential models used: (1) MWPotential2014 (spheroidal bulge, Miyamoto-Nagai disk, NFW halo) implemented in galpy; (2) the non-axisymmetric potential from Law & Majewski (2010) implemented in gala. Both are used for orbit integration to examine relative orbital properties.

source_refs.context: Orbits were integrated in the MWPotential2014 potential.
integrate orbits in the potential that was used in that work ... using gala ... implemented the Law&Majewski2010 potential.
Orbits were integrated in the MWPotential2014 potential ...
we integrate orbits in the potential that was used in that work

method_chain.summary: Integrate orbits of each candidate forward and backward for 1.5 Gyr using galpy (MWPotential2014) and gala (LM10 potential). Compute orbital eccentricity, peri/apocenter distances, and orbital energy.

source_refs.context: We initialize the orbit of each star at its current position and velocity, then integrate its orbit both forward and backward for 1.5 Gyr. From this, we estimate the orbital parameters, including the eccentricity, the peri- and apo-center distances ...

method_chain.summary: Identify one star as a hypervelocity star (HVS) candidate because its orbit integration shows it is unbound from the Milky Way in MWPotential2014. The star has total Galactocentric velocity ~577 km/s and never approaches within 50 kpc of the Galactic center; it does not appear on the eccentricity vs. apocenter plot because it has no measured apocenter (last time step at ~760 kpc).

source_refs.context: HVS subsection describing the star's velocity and unbound status.

method_chain.summary: Discuss possible origins of the HVS candidate (binary ejection, dynamical interaction, dwarf galaxy disruption), ruling out most scenarios based on its orbit and stellar type (early M-giant). Conclude that origin is uncertain and may require refined proper motions.

source_refs.context: Discussion of origin mechanisms for the HVS.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Table 1 of the paper lists 68 chemically selected Sgr stream candidates with membership classifications (a–e), including one hypervelocity star candidate (membership 'd'). The table APOGEE IDs are only partially included in the provided source files; the full catalog is available online.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The full list of APOGEE IDs is not available in the provided source files, so the HVS candidate identifier cannot be extracted individually.

source_refs.context: Table 1 caption and partial rows; footnote * indicates the full list is available from the online journal.
